mechanism_narrative: Sustained elevation of the federal funds rate above 0.5% signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates on equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% as of 2021-09-15 indicates low current tightening pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 147,553 thousand as of 2021-09-15 shows strong employment levels
failure_condition: Negative payroll changes fail to predict negative 5-day returns in > 55% of occurrences, or correlation between PAYEMS momentum and 5-day SPX returns is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales and housing starts signals robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both demand components to strengthen simultaneously to overcome supply constraints.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 625,416 million and HOUST at 1,615 thousand as of 2021-08-15
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with synchronized demand expansion
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in < 55% of triggered instances despite both RSAFS and HOUST accelerating, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in industrial production index signals supply-side weakness and potential stagflationary pressures that compress equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reduce growth expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.5949 as of 2021-08-15 shows near-trend levels
failure_condition: Industrial production declines fail to predict negative 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration of consumer price inflation above 3% year-over-year signals monetary policy lag and real rate compression that forces the Federal Reserve toward tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as equity investors anticipate policy normalization and earnings margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 274.138 as of 2021-09-15 reflects elevated inflation trajectory
failure_condition: CPI acceleration > 3% fails to predict negative 60-day returns in > 58% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate decline below 4.5% combined with accelerating wage growth signals tight labor markets and rising unit labor costs that compress corporate profit margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprice earnings expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.8% and PAYEMS at 147,553 thousand as of 2021-09-15
regime_hint: tight labor market with wage pressure buildup
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 52% of triggered instances despite both UNRATE < 4.5% and PAYEMS accelerating, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured at quarterly frequency above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion that supports equity earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as investors increase growth-oriented positioning.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 19,368.31 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2021-04-15 reflects post-pandemic recovery
failure_condition: Real GDP growth > 2.5% fails to predict positive 60-day returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and declining industrial production (INDPRO) signals stagflationary conditions where supply constraints dominate demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reduce equity risk exposure amid earnings compression and policy uncertainty.
evidence_refs: fact:Elevated inflation with supply-side constraints visible in 2021 data
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in < 54% of triggered instances despite stagflationary signals, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during supply-constrained periods.
